segments.text: After this, Jesus traveled throughout Galilee. He did not want to travel in Judea, because the Jews there were trying to kill Him.
However, the Jewish Feast of Tabernacles was near.
So Jesus’ brothers said to Him,
Leave here and go to Judea, so that Your disciples there may see the works You are doing.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For no one who wants to be known publicly acts in secret. Since You are doing these things, show Yourself to the world.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For even His own brothers did not believe in Him.
Therefore Jesus told them,
Although your time is always at hand, My time has not yet come.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The world cannot hate you, but it hates Me, because I testify that its works are evil.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Go up to the feast on your own. I am not going up to this feast, because My time has not yet come.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Having said this, Jesus remained in Galilee.
But after His brothers had gone up to the feast, He also went—not publicly, but in secret.
So the Jews were looking for Him at the feast and asking,
Where is He?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Many in the crowds were whispering about Him. Some said,
He is a good man.
segments.speaker: Some

segments.text: But others replied,
No, He deceives the people.
segments.speaker: others

segments.text: Yet no one would speak publicly about Him for fear of the Jews.
About halfway through the feast, Jesus went up to the temple courts and began to teach.
The Jews were amazed and asked,
How did this man attain such learning without having studied?
segments.speaker: The Jews were amazed and

segments.text: My teaching is not My own,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied.
It comes from Him who sent Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If anyone desires to do His will, he will know whether My teaching is from God or whether I speak on My own.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He who speaks on his own authority seeks his own glory, but He who seeks the glory of the One who sent Him is a man of truth; in Him there is no falsehood.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Has not Moses given you the law? Yet not one of you keeps it. Why are you trying to kill Me?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You have a demon,
segments.speaker: The crowd

segments.text: the crowd replied.
Who is trying to kill You?
segments.speaker: The crowd

segments.text: Jesus answered them,
I did one miracle, and you are all amazed.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But because Moses gave you circumcision, you circumcise a boy on the Sabbath (not that it is from Moses, but from the patriarchs.)
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If a boy can be circumcised on the Sabbath so that the law of Moses will not be broken, why are you angry with Me for making the whole man well on the Sabbath?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Stop judging by outward appearances, and start judging justly.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then some of the people of Jerusalem began to say,
Isn’t this the man they are trying to kill?
segments.speaker: some of the people of Jerusalem

segments.text: Yet here He is, speaking publicly, and they are not saying anything to Him. Have the rulers truly recognized that this is the Christ?
segments.speaker: some of the people of Jerusalem

segments.text: But we know where this man is from. When the Christ comes, no one will know where He is from.
segments.speaker: some of the people of Jerusalem

segments.text: Then Jesus, still teaching in the temple courts, cried out,
You know Me, and you know where I am from. I have not come of My own accord, but He who sent Me is true. You do not know Him,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: but I know Him, because I am from Him and He sent Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So they tried to seize Him, but no one laid a hand on Him, because His hour had not yet come.
Many in the crowd, however, believed in Him and said,
When the Christ comes, will He perform more signs than this man?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When the Pharisees heard the crowd whispering these things about Jesus, they and the chief priests sent officers to arrest Him.
So Jesus said,
I am with you only a little while longer, and then I am going to the One who sent Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You will look for Me, but you will not find Me; and where I am, you cannot come.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: At this, the Jews said to one another,
Where does He intend to go that we will not find Him? Will He go where the Jews are dispersed among the Greeks,  and teach the Greeks?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: What does He mean by saying, ‘You will look for Me, but you will not find Me,’ and, ‘Where I am, you cannot come’?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: On the last and greatest day of the feast, Jesus stood up and called out in a loud voice,
If anyone is thirsty, let him come to Me and drink.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Whoever believes in Me, as the Scripture has said: ‘Streams of living water will flow from within him.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He was speaking about the Spirit, whom those who believed in Him were later to receive. For the Spirit had not yet been given,  because Jesus had not yet been glorified.
On hearing these words, some of the people said,
This is truly the Prophet.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Others declared,
This is the Christ.
segments.speaker: Others

segments.text: But still others asked,
How can the Christ come from Galilee?
segments.speaker: still others

segments.text: Doesn’t the Scripture say that the Christ will come from the line of David and from Bethlehem, the village where David lived?
segments.speaker: still others

segments.text: So there was division in the crowd because of Jesus.
Some of them wanted to seize Him, but no one laid a hand on Him.

text: The Unbelief of the Jewish Leaders

segments.text: Then the officers returned to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked them,
Why didn’t you bring Him in?
segments.speaker: still others

segments.text: Never has anyone spoken like this man!
segments.speaker: The officers

segments.text: the officers answered.
Have you also been deceived?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: replied the Pharisees.
Have any of the rulers or Pharisees believed in Him?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But this crowd that does not know the law, they are under a curse.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Nicodemus, who had gone to Jesus earlier and who himself was one of them, asked,
Does our law convict a man without first hearing from him to determine what he has done?
segments.speaker: Nicodemus

segments.text: Aren’t you also from Galilee?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they replied.
Look into it, and you will see that no prophet comes out of Galilee.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then each went to his own home.
